message:
feat(extensions): runtime risk controller for extension hostcalls

Add a deterministic, non-LLM runtime risk controller that evaluates
extension hostcall requests against a Bayesian posterior model and
sequential detection process (e-process).  This provides defense-in-depth
for the extension sandbox by dynamically hardening or denying high-risk
operations without requiring model inference.

Core components:
- RuntimeRiskConfig: configurable alpha, window size, ledger limit,
  decision timeout, and fail-closed semantics (disabled by default)
- RuntimeRiskState: per-extension Dirichlet posterior, e-process
  accumulator, conformal residual/drift detection, quarantine tracking
- RuntimeRiskLedgerEntry: hash-chained audit log of every risk decision
  with full posterior/loss/trigger metadata for forensic review
- Three-state model: SafeFast → Suspicious → Unsafe, with corresponding
  actions: Allow → Harden → Deny/Terminate
- Automatic quarantine after consecutive unsafe attempts

Integration points:
- config.rs: ExtensionRiskConfig with env var overrides
  (PI_EXTENSION_RISK_*), merge logic, and resolve_extension_risk()
- agent.rs: wires resolved config into ExtensionManager at session init
- permissions.rs: to_decision_cache() for version-range-aware permission
  retrieval; cache_policy_prompt_decision now stores PersistedDecision
  with version_range so permission decisions are scoped to compatible
  extension versions
- extensions.rs: evaluate_runtime_risk() called in hostcall dispatch
  path; list_permissions() returns full PersistedDecision objects;
  runtime_risk_states cleaned up on extension refresh

Includes three unit tests:
- Disabled controller is isomorphic to no-controller baseline
- Exec calls are denied via risk hardening when enabled
- Repeated unsafe attempts trigger quarantine + terminate action

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
